era_context: Late Predynastic Egypt (Naqada III period). Upper and Lower Egypt were distinct political entities with increasing cultural interaction. Mesopotamia was in the Early Dynastic I period. The Indus Valley Civilization was developing urban centers.
geographic_coverage_gaps: All regions outside Egypt and Mesopotamia
disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are documented for this year.
historiographic_note: 3153 BCE falls in the Predynastic period, entirely predating written Egyptian records. Any historical knowledge of this year is impossible; only archaeological inference about the broader era is possible. The year itself is a modern scholarly convention with no ancient basis.
